description: Design and implement a beautiful, branded HTML email invitation template that hosts can send to guests, replacing plain-text emails with a visually appealing layout that includes event details, cover image, RSVP button, and calendar link.

userStories.title: Create a reusable React Email invitation template component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable HTML email template built with React so that invitation emails render beautifully across all major email clients.
acceptanceCriteria: A new email template module exists at `src/lib/email-templates/invitation.tsx` that exports a function returning an HTML string
The template accepts props: eventTitle, eventDate, eventTime, eventLocation, eventDescription (plain text excerpt), coverImageUrl (optional), rsvpUrl, calendarUrl (optional), hostName
The template uses inline CSS for maximum email client compatibility (no Tailwind classes in the email HTML)
The template includes the Shindig brand color (#7c3aed / purple-600) in the header and CTA button
The layout is responsive and renders correctly at 320px–600px width (standard email viewport)
A prominent 'RSVP Now' call-to-action button links to the guest's unique RSVP URL
The template includes a plain-text fallback version exported as a separate function
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (`npm run build` succeeds)
A helper at `src/lib/email-templates/index.ts` re-exports all templates for clean imports

userStories.title: Integrate the invitation template into the existing email sending flow
userStories.description: As an event host, I want my guests to receive a beautifully designed invitation email when I send invitations so that the event feels professional and exciting.
acceptanceCriteria: The existing invitation send logic in `src/app/api/` routes uses the new HTML template instead of plain text when sending via Resend
The Resend `send()` call includes both `html` (from the template) and `text` (plain-text fallback) fields
The email subject line follows the format: 'You're invited to {eventTitle}!'
The email 'from' address uses the configured Resend sender
Event cover image URL is passed to the template when the event has a cover image
The RSVP URL in the email correctly uses the guest's unique token: `/rsvp/{token}`
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (`npm run build` succeeds)

userStories.title: Add an email preview on the host dashboard
userStories.description: As an event host, I want to preview what the invitation email will look like before sending it so that I can verify the content and design.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Preview Invitation' button appears on the host dashboard event detail page
Clicking the button opens a modal or slide-over panel showing the rendered email template with the current event's data
The preview renders inside an iframe or sandboxed container to simulate email rendering
The preview is responsive — host can toggle between mobile (320px) and desktop (600px) widths
A 'Send Test Email' button in the preview sends the invitation to the host's own email address via a new API route `src/app/api/events/[id]/preview-email/route.ts`
The API route is auth-gated — only the event host can trigger it
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (`npm run build` succeeds)

E2E test in `e2e/email-preview.spec.ts` verifies: preview button renders on dashboard, modal opens with event title visible, mobile/desktop toggle works, and the send-test endpoint returns 200 for authenticated host and 401 for unauthenticated requests
